overview.tagline: A diverse borough with easy access to Manhattan and a growing community
overview.description: Ridgefield is a compact, diverse borough in southern Bergen County situated just west of the Palisades. With a strategic location along Route 1/9 and near the George Washington Bridge, Ridgefield offers convenient access to New York City while maintaining an affordable, suburban environment. The borough features a mix of residential neighborhoods, commercial corridors, and light industrial areas, and has a growing multicultural population that contributes to its vibrant community life.
highlights: Close proximity to George Washington Bridge and NYC
Affordable housing by Bergen County standards
Access to major highways (Route 1/9, I-95, Route 46)

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Ridgefield features a mix of single-family homes, multi-family residences, condominiums, and townhouses.
realEstate.recentTrends: Ridgefield has seen moderate price appreciation, benefiting from its proximity to NYC and relative affordability compared to neighboring towns. The condo and townhouse market is particularly active, attracting young professionals and first-time buyers. Redevelopment projects along commercial corridors are enhancing the borough's appeal.
neighborhoodNotes: Bergen Boulevard area — Commercial corridor with nearby residential streets
Edgewater border — Higher-density residential development near the waterfront
Northern section — Quieter residential neighborhoods toward Palisades Park
Route 1/9 corridor — Commercial and mixed-use areas

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Borough of Ridgefield Department of Public Works.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids, maximum 50 lbs per container
Bulk item pickup available by appointment with DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream curbside recycling is collected weekly.
details: Commingled recyclables collected weekly on designated day
Paper and cardboard collected on the same day
Recycling bins available from the DPW
Yard waste collected seasonally
Electronic waste recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water service is provided by Veolia Water and sewer service is maintained by the Borough.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water (formerly Suez/United Water)
Sewer service maintained by the Borough DPW
Water billing handled by Veolia quarterly
Sewer charges included on property tax bills
Emergency water service available 24/7 through Veolia

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by Planning Board and Zoning Board
Permit applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction and changes of use

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough enforces street parking regulations and operates municipal lots.
details: Municipal parking lots in commercial areas
Street parking regulated by posted signs
Overnight parking restrictions on borough streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Alternate side parking for street cleaning

additionalServices.title: Ridgefield Public Library
additionalServices.description: Public library offering books, digital resources, bilingual programs, and community spaces.
details: Located at 527 Morse Avenue
Children's programs, teen events, and adult programs
ESL and bilingual resources available
Free Wi-Fi and computer access
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for residents of all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues and summer programs
Senior citizen programs and activities
Community events throughout the year
Borough pool open seasonally

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Ridgefield enforces noise regulations to maintain quality of life in residential areas.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise on Sundays or federal holidays
Loud music and amplified sound restricted after 10:00 PM
Commercial noise must not exceed limits in residential zones

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns and yards must be maintained and free of debris
Property owners responsible for sidewalk snow removal within 24 hours
Fences must comply with borough height regulations
Abandoned vehicles must be removed from property
Commercial properties must maintain storefronts and signage

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Ridgefield requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with rabies vaccination proof
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Excessive barking may result in violation notices
Limit on number of pets per household enforced

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking is regulated on borough streets and in commercial areas.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough streets without permit
Alternate side parking enforced for street cleaning
Snow emergency bans require vehicles off-street
Commercial vehicles restricted from residential street parking overnight
Double parking and blocking driveways subject to ticketing

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must be registered and inspected per borough code.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required before occupancy
Landlords must maintain properties to housing code standards
Overcrowding violations enforced based on occupancy limits
Inspections conducted at change of tenancy

other.title: Fence Regulations
other.summary: Fences must comply with borough standards for height, material, and placement.
keyRules: Rear and side yard fences may not exceed 6 feet in height
Front yard fences may not exceed 4 feet in height
Permits required for fence installation
Chain-link fences in front yards may be restricted in certain zones

publicSchools.name: Slocum Skewes Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Primary elementary school serving the borough

publicSchools.name: Shaler Academy (Bergen Arts & Sciences Charter School)
publicSchools.notes: Upper elementary and middle school grades

publicSchools.name: Ridgefield Memorial High School
publicSchools.notes: The borough's high school serving Ridgefield students; also receives students from Edgewater

privateSchools.name: St. Matthew School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic parish school in the Archdiocese of Newark

nearbyHigherEd: New Jersey City University (Jersey City) — 20 minutes

whyMoveHere: Affordable housing compared to neighboring Bergen County communities
Excellent proximity to George Washington Bridge and NYC
Growing, diverse community with multicultural amenities
Access to major highways for commuters
Close to Edgewater waterfront dining and entertainment

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit Bus via Route 1/9 corridor
commuteToNYC.duration: 25-45 minutes to Midtown Manhattan by bus
commuteToNYC.notes: Multiple NJ Transit bus routes provide service to the Port Authority Bus Terminal. The George Washington Bridge is minutes away for drivers. The borough is also near the Edgewater Ferry terminal for service to Manhattan.

costOfLiving.summary: Ridgefield offers one of the more affordable options in Bergen County, with moderate home prices and property taxes compared to neighboring municipalities.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 10% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Affordable housing in a convenient Bergen County location
Very close to NYC via bus and the George Washington Bridge
Proximity to Edgewater waterfront for dining and shopping
Good highway access for commuters (Route 1/9, I-95)
cons: Route 1/9 traffic can be heavy during peak hours
Limited parks and open green space
Schools rated below Bergen County average
Some areas have an industrial or commercial character
Limited nightlife and entertainment options within the borough

restaurants.name: Ridgefield Pizza & Restaurant
restaurants.description: Family-owned pizzeria serving thin-crust pizza, calzones, and Italian-American favorites.

restaurants.name: Corleone's Ristorante
restaurants.description: Italian restaurant offering classic pasta dishes, seafood, and warm hospitality in a neighborhood setting.

restaurants.name: El Paso Mexican Grill
restaurants.description: Casual Mexican restaurant serving tacos, burritos, enchiladas, and other traditional favorites.

restaurants.name: Pho Viet Huong
restaurants.description: Vietnamese restaurant specializing in pho noodle soup, banh mi sandwiches, and traditional Vietnamese cuisine.

parks.name: Veterans Memorial Park
parks.description: Borough park honoring local veterans, featuring walking paths, memorial installations, and green space.

parks.name: Ridgefield Recreation Complex
parks.description: Multi-sport recreation facility with fields, courts, and playground areas for community use.

parks.name: Overpeck County Park (nearby)
parks.description: Large Bergen County park featuring sports fields, walking trails, picnic areas, and a golf course, accessible from Ridgefield.

events.name: Ridgefield Independence Day Celebration
events.description: Annual Fourth of July festivities with live music, food, family activities, and a fireworks display.

events.name: Ridgefield Community Day
events.description: Borough-wide celebration featuring food vendors, entertainment, games, and activities for all ages.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual holiday celebration at Borough Hall with tree lighting, caroling, and refreshments.

attractions.name: Edgewater Waterfront (nearby)
attractions.description: Adjacent Edgewater offers waterfront dining, shopping at The Promenade, and scenic Hudson River views.

attractions.name: Bergen Boulevard Commercial District
attractions.description: The borough's main commercial corridor with a variety of ethnic restaurants, shops, and services.

shoppingAreas: Bergen Boulevard — Main commercial corridor with restaurants and shops
Edgewater Promenade — Waterfront shopping center in adjacent Edgewater
Route 46 shopping — Commercial strip with retail stores
Bergen Town Center (Paramus) — Regional shopping center nearby

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the 2nd and 4th Thursday of each month at 7:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 604 Broad Avenue, Ridgefield, NJ 07657

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
